name: Pomegranate Soup with Meatballs
intro: This is one of my family’s favourite soups. In fact, it’s more hearty than a soup – it is more of a meal in itself. You can, of course, omit the meatballs entirely and opt for a vegetarian version. It’s made both ways in Iran and both are equally delicious, but the most common way to eat it among Persians is with meat, because we do love a good meatball!

ingredients.item: large onions, 2 roughly diced and 1 minced or grated
fat garlic cloves, peeled and crushed
crushed sea salt, plus a little extra for the meatballs
freshly ground black pepper, plus a little extra for the meatballs
large bunch of parsley, leaves and stalks roughly chopped
large bunch of coriander, leaves and stalks roughly chopped
small bunch of mint, leaves roughly chopped
small bunches of chives, roughly chopped
minced lamb (minced beef and veal are also commonly used)

method: Preheat a large cooking pot over a low-medium heat and drizzle in some oil. Add the 2 roughly diced onions and caramelize them. As they start to turn golden, add the garlic and brown gently with the onions. Put in the yellow split peas, pour over the water and bring to the boil. Reduce the heat, partially cover the pan and leave to simmer for 30 minutes over a medium heat.
Add the sea salt and black pepper, turmeric and all the herbs and cook for a further 20 minutes, stirring to prevent the mix from sticking to the bottom of the pan.

To make your meatballs, combine the meat with the minced or grated onion and season generously with some salt and pepper. Roll the mixture into little meatballs about 4cm (1½in) in diameter. Add these to the soup pot along with the uncooked basmati rice, cover and simmer for 30 minutes.

Add the pomegranate juice, pomegranate molasses and sugar and stir the mixture well, then half cover the pan with a lid and simmer for a further 30 minutes. Once cooked, you can adjust the thickness of the soup by adding more hot water, if desired, and also adjust the sugar and pomegranate molasses levels to your taste.
